steps.title: Use future in the past
steps.content: The passage is narrated from a past point: in 1987, Mansour devised the technique. The phrase "Years later" points to an event that was future from that past viewpoint, so the verb needs the future-in-the-past form.

steps.title: Avoid present and prior timing
steps.content: B shifts to simple present tense. C uses ordinary future tense, as if the honors still have not happened. D uses past perfect, placing the honors before the 1987 research despite "Years later." The future-in-the-past form fits. The answer is A.
